title: Indigenous music

infobox.value: is a term for the [traditional music](/wiki/Traditional_music "Traditional music") of the [indigenous peoples](/wiki/Indigenous_peoples "Indigenous peoples") of the world, that is, the music of an "original" [ethnic group](/wiki/Ethnic_group "Ethnic group") that inhabits any geographic region alongside more recent immigrants who may be greater in number.[[1]](#cite_note-1) The term therefore depends upon the political role an ethnic group plays rather than upon its strictly musical characteristics, for all further criteria (territory, race, history, subsistence lifestyle, etc.) defining indigenous peoples can also be applied to majority cultures.[[2]](#cite_note-FAQ-2)
Peoples of the world by National Geographic Society

["Frequently Asked Questions: Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples"](https://www.un.org/esa/socdev/unpfii/documents/FAQsindigenousdeclaration.pdf) (PDF). *United Nations Permanent Forum on Indigenous Issues*. Retrieved 2009-10-23.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Music of indigenous ethnic groups

**Indigenous music** is a term for the [traditional music](/wiki/Traditional_music "Traditional music") of the [indigenous peoples](/wiki/Indigenous_peoples "Indigenous peoples") of the world, that is, the music of an "original" [ethnic group](/wiki/Ethnic_group "Ethnic group") that inhabits any geographic region alongside more recent immigrants who may be greater in number.[[1]](#cite_note-1) The term therefore depends upon the political role an ethnic group plays rather than upon its strictly musical characteristics, for all further criteria (territory, race, history, subsistence lifestyle, etc.) defining indigenous peoples can also be applied to majority cultures.[[2]](#cite_note-FAQ-2)

Societies defined as, or defining themselves as, "indigenous" are found in every inhabited [climate zone](/wiki/Climate_zone "Climate zone") and [continent](/wiki/Continent "Continent") of the world.[[3]](#cite_note-3) Some important articles are:

* [Music of Africa](/wiki/Music_of_Africa "Music of Africa"), especially the non-[European](/wiki/Europe "Europe"), [Asian](/wiki/Asian_people "Asian people") or [Arab](/wiki/Arab "Arab")-derived traditions
* [Māori music](/wiki/Maori_music "Maori music") [of New Zealand](/wiki/Music_of_New_Zealand "Music of New Zealand")
* [Music of the Aborigines and Torres Strait Islanders](/wiki/Indigenous_Australian_music "Indigenous Australian music") [of Australia](/wiki/Music_of_Australia "Music of Australia")
* Music of the indigenous peoples of [Latin America](/wiki/Music_of_Latin_America "Music of Latin America") and [the Caribbean](/wiki/Caribbean_music "Caribbean music")
* [Native American music](/wiki/Native_American_music "Native American music") of the United States and [Inuit](/wiki/Inuit "Inuit"), [Métis](/wiki/M%C3%A9tis_people_(Canada) "Métis people (Canada)") and [First Nation music](/wiki/Aboriginal_music_of_Canada "Aboriginal music of Canada") of Canada
* [Sámi music](/wiki/S%C3%A1mi_music "Sámi music") of [Norway](/wiki/Music_of_Norway "Music of Norway"), [Sweden](/wiki/Music_of_Sweden "Music of Sweden"), [Finland](/wiki/Music_of_Finland "Music of Finland"), and [Russia](/wiki/Music_of_Russia "Music of Russia")
* Music of [South India](/wiki/South_India "South India")
* Music of the [republics of Russia](/wiki/Republics_of_Russia "Republics of Russia")
